title: SMOVE
description: Moves a member from one set to another.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information. Move `member` from the set at `source` to the set at `destination`. This operation is atomic. In every given moment the element will appear to be a member of `source` **or** `destination` for other clients. If the source set does not exist or does not contain the specified element, no operation is performed and `0` is returned. Otherwise, the element is removed from the source set and added to the destination set. When the specified element already exists in the destination set, it is only removed from the source set. ## Examples SADD myset "one" SADD myset "two" SADD myotherset "three" SMOVE myset myotherset "two" SMEMBERS myset SMEMBERS myotherset ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** One of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the element is moved. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if the element is not a member of _source_ and no operation was performed. **RESP3:** One of the following: * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the element is moved. * [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if the element is not a member of _source_ and no operation was performed.
intro:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.



Move `member` from the set at `source` to the set at `destination`.
This operation is atomic.
In every given moment the element will appear to be a member of `source` **or**
`destination` for other clients.

If the source set does not exist or does not contain the specified element, no
operation is performed and `0` is returned.
Otherwise, the element is removed from the source set and added to the
destination set.
When the specified element already exists in the destination set, it is only
removed from the source set.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.



Move `member` from the set at `source` to the set at `destination`.
This operation is atomic.
In every given moment the element will appear to be a member of `source` **or**
`destination` for other clients.

If the source set does not exist or does not contain the specified element, no
operation is performed and `0` is returned.
Otherwise, the element is removed from the source set and added to the
destination set.
When the specified element already exists in the destination set, it is only
removed from the source set.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
SADD myset "one"
SADD myset "two"
SADD myotherset "three"
SMOVE myset myotherset "two"
SMEMBERS myset
SMEMBERS myotherset

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

One of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the element is moved.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if the element is not a member of _source_ and no operation was performed.

**RESP3:**

One of the following:
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `1` if the element is moved.
* [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): `0` if the element is not a member of _source_ and no operation was performed.
